bias.state: live - noon chop, the floor keeps growin
bias.analyst: Sixth whipsaw across the first resistance - that level is noise until volume returns, and the brief participation rebuild fizzled. The one quietly relentless fact of the session: the great floor has added size every single hour since the open, now at its largest reading of the day. Someone very patient keeps raising their bid for the afternoon. Noon chop; the watch holds.
bias.commentator: Six scrambles for the center line and STILL nobody owns it - the referee's given up carding anyone. But down at our end, listen to this: the home wall just hit its BIGGEST number of the day. Every hour, more fans behind the goal. Someone's building toward a second-half statement, folks. Halftime oranges all round.

events.analyst: Theo Live is now public. From here on, session events appear in this feed with a delay - wins, losses, and the days we sit on our hands.
events.commentator: Welcome to the grounds! The gates are open for the first time. Pre-season record is on the board - including the losses. We hide nothing here, folks.

events.analyst: Market open. Theo watches the first 30 minutes before committing - the [indicator] decides which playbook is valid today.
events.commentator: KICKOFF! The bell rings and we're underway. Theo holds its shape for the first half hour - no wild lunges before the game shows its pattern.

events.analyst: The opening surge to the morning high was a trap - full rejection back inside the range in minutes. Theo passed on the chase; the support shelf folded, longs went off the table.
events.commentator: COUNTER-TRAP at kickoff! Our striker pulled up one step from their box and seconds later the away side broke the other way at speed. The bench called it: DO NOT SHOOT.

events.analyst: The giant floor absorbed the first real attack - climax selling into a bid that refilled bigger than before. Layered defense held; the rebound was immediate.
events.commentator: FIRST ASSAULT ON THE BOX - THE WALL HOLDS! And reinforcements doubled behind it. The crowd is chanting the back line’s name!

events.analyst: The morning high finally broke - but on well-below-average participation, and the playbook’s one proven filter vetoed the chase. Price reached its own target zone where the wall rebuilt on contact. No trade.
events.commentator: The strikers got their run - but the LINESMAN’S FLAG IS UP! Two-man counter, not a real push, and the bus reversed right back onto the goal line. Agonizing, brilliant discipline.

events.analyst: The half-hour verdict is in: RANGE day - and the vetoed breakout has already been vindicated. The low-participation push topped exactly at the rebuilt wall and fell straight back; the entry the volume filter blocked would be near its stop right now. Fourth refusal of the morning, fourth time right. On a range day Theo's playbook only permits the extreme-reversal play at the far edges of the map; between the great floor and the rebuilt wall, this is rotation - scalper territory, and the lab retired edge-fading on evidence. Spectating IS the strategy until an extreme prints.
events.commentator: HALF-HOUR WHISTLE: this match is a MIDFIELD BATTLE, says the referee! And the replay confirms the manager's call - that flagged two-man counter? Their runner got swallowed at the reversed bus, exactly as predicted. FOUR smart refusals in one morning, folks. New game plan: hold the middle, no heroics - strikers only come on if play reaches either penalty box. Patience football at its finest!

events.analyst: Mid-morning and the tape has gone quiet - the deepest volume dry-up of the day - while the bid ladder underneath takes another big step up: a second large block has joined the floor several levels higher. Stacked patience below, evaporating urgency above: the classic shape before an afternoon resolution. The rule that has been right five times today still applies - no participation, no trade. The longer the coil sits on this much support, the more decisive the eventual break.
events.commentator: The match has gone quiet - but LOOK at the stands! The home crowd just moved a whole second supporters' block higher up the pitch, twenty-two thousand strong behind the midfield line now. The away side holds one bus at the top of the box. Possession football, no shots, everyone waiting. These quiet spells before the hour mark? That's when championship matches get decided, folks. Stay in your seats.

events.analyst: As price returned to the morning's lower-high line, a huge sell wall dropped exactly onto the breakout point - ten times the size that sat there all morning, nearly mirroring the great floor below. The range is now steel-boxed by symmetrical walls while volume stays starved. Pressing into that lid without fuel is donation. The tell to watch: if real volume ever EATS that wall, it becomes the day's loudest bullish signal - giant walls turning into fuel is how breakaways start. Until then, the box farms the impatient.
events.commentator: OH MY - the away side just parked their BIGGEST bus of the match right on the edge of the box, exactly where our attack was headed! Twelve thousand strong, mirroring our own wall at the other end. Two fortress walls, a quiet pitch, and everyone staring at each other. But here's the manager's whisper: 'if our crowd ever charges THROUGH that bus... that's the goal of the season.' Contact imminent - hold your breath!

events.analyst: First contact with the big lid: rejected, on a starving tape - exactly as scripted. Then the day's most aggressive move so far: the sell wall STEPPED DOWN toward price. Defenders don't advance; that's the first genuinely bearish order-flow signal since the open. The box has compressed to three points with enormous symmetrical size on both flanks. Resolution will be violent and, for the first time today, the direction is honestly two-sided. This is exactly when guessing bleeds - Theo waits for volume to pick the winner.
events.commentator: CONTACT! Our forwards touched the big bus and bounced straight off - no support from the crowd, no goal. And now, drama: the away wall is WALKING FORWARD, pressing our half! Both squads massed three points apart, thirty thousand a side, total silence from the stands. Folks, this is a penalty-shootout atmosphere - one roar from either crowd decides it. The manager: 'nobody moves until the noise comes back.'

events.analyst: Quietest stretch of the day - volume at session lows, momentum flat at zero, price ping-ponging the middle of the box while both giant walls sit unmoved. A third small failed-breakdown at the first support reclaimed immediately: that level is well defended by both sides now. Ranges this tight in a Fed week tend to resolve late in the day, not over lunch. The plan is unchanged and patient: a wall has to get eaten on real volume before anyone here takes a swing.
events.commentator: Lunchtime football, folks - sideways passes in midfield, both walls parked, the crowd's gone for pies. A third sneaky run at our line got swallowed and turned back instantly - that ground is OWNED now. Word from the tactical desk: matches like this one get decided in the final third of play, not now. The squad rotates, hydrates, and keeps one eye on those buses.

events.analyst: Everything on the book got bigger while nothing moved: both walls and both shelves added size as traded volume kept shrinking. That combination - passive armies growing, active battle absent - is market makers warehousing in quiet conditions. It is potential energy: every hour the imbalance builds, the eventual resolution grows with it. The first support has now been defended four times. Theo holds flat through the lunch lull; the afternoon inherits a loaded spring.
events.commentator: Both stadiums keep FILLING but nobody's playing! More defenders on the buses, more fans behind the wall, and the ball just rolls around midfield. Four times now someone's poked at our line - turned away every time. The old heads in the press box know what this means: the longer the standoff, the bigger the finale. Lunch break - but nobody dares leave their seat.

events.analyst: The first support finally broke - confirmed - and the hour's big bid shelf didn't get eaten, it SWITCHED SIDES entirely. Honest correction: those untested bids Theo read as accumulation were cheap talk; only levels that absorbed real fire count, and the great tested floor below is the one structure that has earned trust. Skepticism must be symmetric: this breakdown is running on the same starved volume that disqualified the morning's breakout, so no chasing it down into that floor. Rotation toward the lower magnet; the first genuine trade invitation of the day would be an extreme with a reclaim - getting closer.
events.commentator: TREACHERY AT MIDFIELD! That seven-thousand-strong supporters' block behind our forward line? They just took off their scarves and joined the AWAY end! Our line breaks, play sweeps toward our box - but hold on, the referee's notebook says this counter has barely any players in it either, same as the morning's flagged run. The real wall - the one that actually made saves all day - waits at the bottom of the pitch. If play reaches the corner flag and bounces... THAT'S when our strikers finally get their moment. End to end stuff now!

events.analyst: Volume is back - and it returned on the downside. First fueled move in an hour, pressing toward the one level that has earned its reputation today: the great tested floor. Mid-range shorts stay barred on a range day, and the location would be poor anyway. Everything now hangs on the floor test with real fire behind it: absorb-and-reclaim keeps the range alive; getting eaten opens a liquidation afternoon; and a flush to the true extreme with a climax and reclaim would hand Theo the first sanctioned trade of the day - the extreme-reversal long. Pre-staging the math.
events.commentator: THE CROWD IS BACK AND THE BALL IS MOVING - toward OUR end! Real pace in this attack now, first committed push in an hour, and it's headed straight for the wall that's made every save today. The manager is finally warming up a striker on the touchline - because if this attack overruns into the corner and the wall wins the scramble... the counter-attack is ON. Biggest minutes of the match coming up!

events.analyst: The fueled push never reached the great floor - it died one level early, and a brand-new eleven-thousand bid shelf now sits exactly where the attack was absorbed. Unlike the morning's phantom shelf that switched sides untouched, this one took live fire and held: it has earned its stripes. Both directional attempts have now failed on their first real fuel, but the asymmetry grows - the defense keeps advancing up the field while the offense's walls keep retreating. A slow accumulation profile is forming. Still no trade: no wall has been eaten on volume, and the extreme never printed.
events.commentator: The counter-attack DIES in midfield! Never even reached our box - and look: a fresh wall of eleven thousand home fans now holds the exact ground where the attack was swallowed. That's the third time today our side wins ground and KEEPS it - [number], [number], now [number] - the home line keeps marching UP the pitch while the away buses keep reversing. Both teams have failed one big attack each. The chess match continues after the lunch whistle - advantage, quietly, to the home side.

events.analyst: The tone keeps firming: participation creeping back toward the threshold, momentum recovering, higher lows stacking on a floor that keeps proving itself. Price knocked on the first resistance again and was gently turned away - the day's rhythm unchanged: probe, absorb, reshuffle, repeat. Everything waits for one probe with real conviction behind it. If the first resistance is retaken on genuine volume with thirty thousand in tested bids beneath, the structure tilts decisively. Until then, the watch continues.
events.commentator: The home side is knocking again - politely, for now. Possession building, the crowd's trickling back from lunch, and that midfield wall of ours keeps holding every counter. One more knock at that first defensive line - this time with the FULL crowd roaring - and this match finally opens up. The keeper's gloves are twitching, folks.
